text.title: GraphQL Schema Patterns
text.summary: SDL structure, type conventions, cursor-based pagination, subscriptions, and federation key declarations.

rule.statement: Define Query, Mutation, and Subscription root types; group related fields with tags and input types for all mutations.
rule.rationale: Input types allow validation, evolution without breaking clients, and consistent mutation signatures.

rule.statement: All list fields use Relay cursor-based pagination (Connection/Edge/PageInfo) instead of offset pagination.
rule.rationale: Offset pagination produces duplicates and gaps when items are inserted/deleted between pages. Cursor pagination is stable under concurrent writes.

rule.statement: In federated graphs, declare entity types with @key(fields: "id") in the owning subgraph, and extend with @external in consuming subgraphs.
rule.rationale: Federation allows multiple services to contribute fields to the same type while each service owns its domain.
